meta.title: Part 5 — extra practice set (15 items)
meta.notes: Extra bank for app; verify answers before study.

items.prompt: The vendor agreed to ------- the delivery schedule after the port reopened.
items.explanation: Agree to + bare infinitive revise; revision is noun; revises wrong after to.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item tests the blank where wording around vendor and agreed plus delivery from the same sentence forces one licensed choice among the four listed options.
Anchor on "The vendor agreed to" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside vendor in context.
A tempting trap is option C when "revising" sounds plausible near agreed, yet it does not lock onto the same clause pattern the sentence builds around delivery.
Option B with "revises" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "revision" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "revise" for this gap; compare "revises" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to vendor here.

items.prompt: The committee will not ------- the merger until regulators publish their guidance.
items.explanation: Will not + base verb finalize before object merger.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item tests the blank where wording around committee and merger plus until from the same sentence forces one licensed choice among the four listed options.
Anchor on "The committee will not" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside committee in context.
A tempting trap is option C when "finalized" sounds plausible near merger, yet it does not lock onto the same clause pattern the sentence builds around until.
Option B with "finalizing" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "finalization" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "finalize" for this gap; compare "finalizing" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to committee here.

items.prompt: ------- the pilot exceeded expectations, the steering group approved a second funding round.
items.explanation: Although + clause shows concession before main clause approval.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Connector item tests the blank where wording around pilot and exceeded plus expectations from the same sentence forces one licensed choice among the four listed options.
Anchor on "the pilot exceeded expectati" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside pilot in context.
A tempting trap is option B when "Despite" sounds plausible near exceeded, yet it does not lock onto the same clause pattern the sentence builds around expectations.
Option A with "Because" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "Unless" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike C.
The correct string is "Although" for this gap; compare "Because" from A which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to pilot here.

items.prompt: The warehouse must remain ------- during the inspection, so all forklifts are idled.

Anchor on "The warehouse must remain" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside warehouse in context.

Option B with "operationally" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "operate" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "operational" for this gap; compare "operationally" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to warehouse here.

items.prompt: The revised handbook clarifies that contractors ------- wear badges in secured zones.
items.explanation: Mandatory rule: must wear; might/could too weak for compliance text.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Tense item tests the blank where wording around revised and handbook plus clarifies from the same sentence forces one licensed choice among the four listed options.
Anchor on "clarifies that contractors" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside revised in context.
A tempting trap is option C when "could" sounds plausible near handbook, yet it does not lock onto the same clause pattern the sentence builds around clarifies.
Option B with "might" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "would" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "must" for this gap; compare "might" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to revised here.

items.prompt: The finance team attributed the variance ------- a timing difference in revenue recognition.

items.prompt: Shipping costs have risen ------- fuel surcharges imposed by carriers last quarter.
items.explanation: Owing to + noun phrase fuel surcharges; because needs a clause.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Connector item tests the blank where wording around shipping and costs plus risen from the same sentence forces one licensed choice among the four listed options.
Anchor on "Shipping costs have risen" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside shipping in context.
A tempting trap is option C when "although" sounds plausible near costs, yet it does not lock onto the same clause pattern the sentence builds around risen.
Option B with "because" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "unless" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "owing to" for this gap; compare "because" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to shipping here.

items.prompt: The board deferred the vote ------- the legal memo clears every outstanding liability clause.

items.prompt: The memo reminds staff that confidential files ------- on encrypted drives only.

Anchor on "aff that confidential files" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside memo in context.
